source_jobs.title: Broker Business Development Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Skip
Skip is a mission-driven Australian home loan lender built to fast-track Aussies into home ownership. We help Australians ‘skip to the owning bit’ with low deposit home loans. Buying a home shouldn’t mean putting life on pause to save a massive deposit while property prices sprint ahead. We’re here to build a fairer, faster path to home ownership, so more people can stop paying their landlord’s mortgage and start paying their own.

We’re backed by leading US venture funds, global credit partners, and prominent Australian investors including Daniel Petre AO and Ed Robinson. We originate low-deposit home loans and are scaling our loan book aggressively, with our loan book being multiples larger than last year. We have major distribution partnerships live, strong credit performance, and expanding product lines - we’re entering a true hyper-growth phase.

We’re a team that moves quickly, obsesses over the customer experience, and backs each other to do the best work of our careers. If you like solving meaningful problems, shipping real change, and taking ownership, you’ll feel at home here.


Mission of the Broker team
The mission of the Broker BDM team is to acquire, activate, and grow high-quality mortgage broker partnerships by positioning Skip as a trusted, go-to low-deposit lending solution. You are responsible for turning new broker relationships into long-term advocates who consistently place loan volume, driving sustainable growth across your territory.

You win by: opening doors, educating with confidence, building trust fast, relentlessly moving deals through the pipeline.



About the role

As a Broker Business Development Manager, you’ll be the face of Skip to mortgage brokers in the region — helping them understand our product, trust our model, and become long-term advocates sending consistent loan volume our way.

We’re looking for smart, hungry salespeople ready to build something big. You’re a ‘Hunter’. You pride yourself on building new relationships and ringing the bell.

What you do need: a track record of winning, building relationships, and driving growth in fast-paced industries like SaaS, tech, or other high-performance sales environments.



What will you be doing on a day to day basis?

 - Managing and growing your broker pipeline

 - Learning and understanding our credit policy and eligibility criteria back-to-front

 - Pitching and educating brokers on Skip’s low-deposit home loan products.

 - Building meaningful relationships and turning brokers into advocates.

 - Managing Loan Scenarios and pushing deals through the pipeline

 - Representing Skip at industry events and partner sessions.

 - Drive awareness and engagement across your territory — get the word out.

 - Running the role on scenarios: scenarios are the best leading indicator of performance / deals, measured weekly, logged in Attio, with targets tied to that leading indicator not just settled loans.

 - Focus and depth: You will concentrate on T1 brokers, and grow them from 1-deal to 3–5+



We're looking for someone who is:

 - A proven sales performer with 2–5 years’ experience (B2B, SaaS, tech, or similar).

 - Confident with technology and highly adept with CRM, automation, and sales technology.

 - Curious, ambitious, and confident presenting to people.

 - A natural hunter — loves creating new relationships and closing opportunities.

 - Comfortable owning targets and managing a sales cycle end-to-end.

 - Highly organised with strong CRM discipline.

 - Motivated by uncapped earnings and making a tangible impact

 - Able to understand credit policy as a core competency, not background, it's what earns trust and converts scenarios.

 - Bring high-output effort, ownership, coachability, resilience.



Why join us?

 - Competitive Rewards - We offer competitive salary packages plus an Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP), so you don’t just work here — you own a part of what we’re building

 - Do Your Best Work - We create space

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Mission:

We build the machine that builds the machine.

Product and Platform development - we're growing fast. What we build is critical for ensuring we can meet demand of our customers, enabling everyone in the business to be 100x more impactful in what they do. Ensuring our Product and Platform are built for scale and extensibility is crucial, both from a human and technical standpoint.

The Self Driving Code Base - AI leveraged development is in, shipping slop is not. Designing the primitives and guardrails that allow us to ship more with increasing levels of quality is now part of the gig.



About the role:

We're hiring builders: curious, technical generalists who can think across disciplines and ship end-to-end.

At Skip, there's no handoff between "product" and "engineering." You'll talk to ops, sales and product to understand the business constraint, build it, ship it, and iterate. You won't be handed a spec. You'll be the person who writes the spec and the code — by default, you'll direct AI to write the code for you.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Own outcomes, not tickets. You'll take a business problem and turn it into a working, scalable system.

 - Ship product. We care about what's live and working, not what's in a design doc. You'll ship frequently and iterate based on real usage from our customers or team.

 - Make product decisions. You'll scope what to build (and what not to build), sequence work, and make trade-offs between speed, quality, and complexity. You'll talk directly to the people who use what you build

 - Create solid code. Our stack is React, Node, TypeScript, AWS. You'll create production code that other engineers can extend and follow - not prototypes that need to be rewritten.

 - Direct AI to build. We're an AI-native engineering team. You'll use agent-first workflows to ship at a pace that would be impossible manually. The bottleneck isn't implementation — it's judgment about what to build and why.

 - Prioritisation - While you’ll be responsible for making product decisions and moving quickly, you’ll need to work with the team to ensure your velocity is always pointed in the right direction and aligned to our commercial goals.
   

What you will bring:

 - A curious generalist, not a narrow specialist. You cross disciplines naturally. You've done product thinking, systems design, user research, or ops problem-solving - not because it was in your job description, but because you wanted to understand the whole problem.

 - Product instinct. You naturally ask "should we build this?" before "how do we build this?" You can talk to a non-technical stakeholder, understand their pain, and translate it into the right technical solution.

 - End-to-end ownership. You've shipped features or systems where you were the person responsible for the outcome — from problem definition through to production and iteration. You've built a tonne from scratch, not just inherited and extended.

 - Strong technical fundamentals. You write clean, maintainable code, understand systems design, and make pragmatic architecture decisions.

 - Comfort with ambiguity. We're a small team at a growth-stage fintech. Requirements are often fuzzy. You're energised by that, not paralysed.

 - Clear communication. You write well. You can explain a technical trade-off to a founder and a product decision to an engineer.

 - You're in it for the love of the game - you love building. You are a technical builder who loves solving problems, if “software engineering” to you is just a “good job” - this probably isn’t the shop for you.
   

Why join us:

 - Shape what building software looks like in the AI era

 - Remote flexibility

 - Competitive remuneration & ESOP - we want you invested in Skip's success

 - Work on meaningful problems - helping more Australians access home ownership sooner

 - High agency, high ownership environment where strong operators can make a visible impact

 - Opportunity to lead operational transformation du

source_jobs.title: Lending Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Mission:

Customer-centricity is core to Skip’s values and mission, and at no other point during the home loan journey is it more critical than at settlement. When the buzz of signing a contract and receiving unconditional approval wears off, settlement can be a daunting time for customers as they navigate the complexities of legal documents, condition satisfaction, upcoming deadlines and a multitude of stakeholders. Our Operations team is responsible for ensuring that our customers have a best-in-class experience by staying supported, informed and nurtured - throughout both the settlement process and the full borrower lifecycle.



About the role:

This is a high-impact, end-to-end mortgage operations role spanning settlements and servicing across the full loan lifecycle.

From the moment a loan becomes unconditional, you step in as the driving force behind execution. You ensure progress never stalls; deadlines are met without compromise; and every stakeholder knows exactly what needs to happen next - whether that be brokers, customers, solicitors, our funder, legal partners or internal teams. These are high-value, time-sensitive transactions and you take personal responsibility for keeping them on track.

Post-settlement, you’ll continue to grow relationships with the same customers you onboarded by supporting them across servicing requests, loan variations and discharges. With loan volumes growing rapidly, this role is critical to ensuring Skip maintains speed, precision and an exceptional customer experience.



Key Responsibilities:

Settlements (Pre-Settlement to Completion)

 - Own the end-to-end settlement process from unconditional approval to successful settlement

 - Liaise with brokers, customers, solicitors, funders, and legal partners to coordinate timelines and documents

 - Identify and resolve risks or issues prior to settlement by managing documentation, condition satisfaction and funding requirements

 - Book and confirm settlements, manage fund disbursements and ensure accuracy of settlement figures

 - Ensure all internal and external stakeholders are proactively updated on progress



Loan Servicing (Post-Settlement Lifecycle)

 - Manage onboarding and post-settlement customer communications/enquiries

 - Process servicing requests including direct debit amendments, loan statements, redraws and account maintenance

 - Manage dishonours and arrears by engaging with customers

 - Process loan payouts and discharges

 - Maintain accurate records across loan and CRM systems to ensure data integrity



Operational Excellence

 - Contribute to improving workflows, automation and system enhancements

 - Ensure SLAs and performance metrics are consistently met

 - Assist in building scalable processes as loan volumes grow using AI and automation



What you will bring:

 - 2-4 years’ experience in mortgage settlements, loan servicing, credit support or lender operations

 - Strong understanding of the Australian mortgage lifecycle (post-approval functions essential)

 - Working knowledge of NCCP, AML/CTF and responsible lending obligations (preferred)

 - Exceptional attention to detail and time management skills

 - Clear, confident stakeholder communication (brokers, solicitors, customers, funders)

 - High ownership mindset - you run your settlement matters end-to-end

 - Calm under pressure in time-sensitive settlement environments

 - Process-oriented but improvement-driven - you look for and recommend better ways to do things



Why join us:

 - Real career growth - we’re a fast-growing company, which means genuine opportunities to grow your career as we scale

 - Competitive remuneration & ESOP - we want you invested in Skip's success

 - Be part of building a first-class mortgage operations function

 - Work in a high-ownership environment where your impact is visible and recognised

 - Exposure across the full loan lifecycle that allows you to develop strong customer relationships

 - Collaborative, high

source_jobs.title: Credit Risk Manager
source_jobs.description:
Mission:

To build and lead Skip’s second-line Credit Risk function – the independent check that keeps our credit quality, portfolio performance and regulatory standing strong as we scale the loan book many times over. This role exists to turn credit risk from something that lives in one or two people’s heads into a real, evidenced, scalable function: clear risk appetite, sharp policy, robust assurance and QA, and reporting that our board, funders and regulators can trust.

You will be the person who owns how Skip thinks about and controls credit risk day to day – working closely with our Head of Credit & Underwriting, a 30-year credit and LMI veteran, who will act as your mentor and advisor as you grow into the full breadth of the function over time.

 

About the role:

Skip has deep credit seniority at an advisory level, but we have not yet built out the operating engine of a modern credit risk function – the frameworks, assurance, QA, monitoring and reporting that let a small team hold the line on quality while the business grows fast. That is what you will own and build.

You will sit in the second line of defence, independent of day-to-day credit operations (the first line). Where the Credit Operations team owns getting applications assessed and decisions made, you own the framework those decisions are made within, and the independent assurance that they are sound: risk appetite, credit policy, delegated authority calibration, QA and assurance, portfolio monitoring, and credit risk reporting.

You will report to the Head of Operations, with a dotted line to our Head of Credit & Underwriting as your mentor and to the Credit Committee, to whom the second-line function ultimately provides independent assurance.

Most importantly, this is a role with real room to grow. We are deliberately hiring someone earlier in their leadership journey who has genuine credit depth and the ambition to build. You will start by standing up the core of the function, and as you prove yourself, your mandate, authority and scope will expand – with a path to owning the function over time. We want someone who sees the gap between where our credit risk function is today and where it needs to be at 10x and is energised by closing it.

This is a role for someone with sound credit judgment, the structure and discipline to build a function from first principles, and the ownership mindset to treat Skip’s credit quality as their own.

 

Key responsibilities:

Credit Risk Framework & Policy

 - Own and continuously improve Skip’s credit risk framework – risk appetite, credit policy, lending standards and the delegated lending authority (DLA) framework – working with the Head of Credit & Underwriting to translate his policy intent into clear, usable, well-governed documents.

 - Keep policy fit-for-stage and defendable: aligned to responsible lending obligations, our funder and warehouse requirements, and Skip’s risk appetite – neither gold-plated nor loose.

 - Own policy governance — the end-to-end framework for how credit policy is proposed, reviewed, approved, and version-controlled.

 - Own policy change rollout — drive implementation of approved changes end-to-end, including communication, calibration, and ongoing monitoring post-launch

 - Own second-line oversight of fraud, AML/CTF, and KYC controls - including PEP and sanctions screening - acting as the escalation point for suspicious matches, high-risk cases, and control breaches, and ensuring timely, well-documented resolution in line with Skip's AML/CTF Program and reporting obligations to AUSTRAC.

Assurance, QA & Decision Quality

 - Build and run Skip’s second-line credit assurance and QA program – independent review of credit decisions and DLA usage to test that the first line is decisioning within policy and appetite.

 - Calibrate and monitor DLA decisioning across the team, surfacing inconsistency, drift or emerging risk early and feeding it back into policy, training and process.

 - E
